news.t: Australia's Consumer Inflation Jumps on Oil Shock
Australia reports lower-than-expected first-quarter inflation — but price rise highest in 2 years
S&P Global downgrades ASX after Australian regulator finds governance, risk failures
Australia's Growth Accelerates, Bolstering Case for RBA to Raise Rates
Australia raises rates for first time since late 2023 as inflation hits six-quarter high
Cautious RBA in No Rush to Cut Rates Further
Australian Inflation Indicator Jumps, Backing RBA Caution on Rate Cuts

holdings.infoBox: FLAU is an equity ETF with a total of 111 individual holdings. The top holdings are BHP Group stock at 13.12%, Commonwealth Bank of Australia at 11.34%, Westpac Banking at 5.09%, National Australia Bank at 4.75%, and ANZ Group Holdings at 4.38%.

overview.description: The Franklin FTSE Australia ETF (FLAU) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the FTSE Australia RIC Capped index. The fund tracks a market cap-weighted index of large- and mid-cap Australian stocks. FLAU was launched on Nov 2, 2017 and is issued by Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Australia's Consumer Inflation Jumps on Oil Shock
data.text: Australia's consumer prices jumped in the first quarter, hitting the highest level since September 2023, as the impact of the energy shock fed into the data.

data.title: Australia reports lower-than-expected first-quarter inflation — but price rise highest in 2 years
data.text: Inflation hit it highest level since the fourth quarter of 2023. RBA signaled inflation would stay above its 2%–3% target for longer.

data.title: S&P Global downgrades ASX after Australian regulator finds governance, risk failures
data.text: S&P Global Ratings on Thursday lowered its issuer credit rating for the Australian Securities Exchange to "A+/A-1" from "AA-/A-1+", two weeks after a local regulator flagged ​governance and risk manag...

data.title: Australia's Growth Accelerates, Bolstering Case for RBA to Raise Rates
data.text: The growth data follows a monthly inflation report that showed price pressures continued to build in the Australian economy.

data.title: Australia raises rates for first time since late 2023 as inflation hits six-quarter high
data.text: Australia's central bank raised its policy rate by 25 basis points to 3.85%. That marked the Reserve Bank of Australia's first rate hike since November 2023.

data.title: Cautious RBA in No Rush to Cut Rates Further
data.text: The Reserve Bank of Australia said future policy decisions will remain dependent on the flow of economic data.

data.title: Australian Inflation Indicator Jumps, Backing RBA Caution on Rate Cuts
data.text: Australia's monthly inflation indicator jumped to its highest level in a year in July, affirming the Reserve Bank of Australia's cautious approach to cutting interest rates over recent months.
